programme.title: Defenders UK

programme.title: Doctors

programme.title: Impossible

programme.title: Escape to the Country

programme.title: Garden Rescue

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Angellica Bell host the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Inside Out South
programme.desc: Jon Cuthill investigates a Gosport firm that has left owners of small businesses out of pocket and tied to long, expensive phone contracts. The programme also follows 17-year-old American football player Paul Fifield from Reading as he joins hundreds of youngsters hoping to be chosen for one of just 80 places at the NFL's new UK sports academy(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The events of last week continue to hang heavy in Walford. Chantelle passes her work assessment with flying colours but as the family celebrates Gray seems determined to sabotage the event. Tina is sorely tempted when Shirley tells her about a job she is doing for Phil abroad and asks her to come along(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Growing numbers of young people are carrying knives and becoming victims of knife crime, while doctors report that the injuries from knives they are treating in hospital are becoming more severe and the victims getting younger. In this programme, Chanell Wallace, whose own brother was stabbed to death, meets young people growing up in communities where carrying a knife is normal, sees how violence from knife crime is turning lives upside down and spends time in a school and college to see the impact of knives on the classroom(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: This second heat week sees five more celebrities enter the MasterChef kitchen. with Dom Parker, Josie Long, Mim Shaikh, Jenny Ryan and Alex George facing three culinary challenges. They first tackle an invention test, before they are split into two groups to take on the challenge of cooking for the first time in a restaurant kitchen, preparing dishes for paying customers. In the final task they are asked to prepare a dish of their own design in just one hour, before judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace decide which of the contestants will leave the competition(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: The vicar's son returns to the village and has to readjust to rural life having lived in Bristol, while Kerry tries to give back to the community by visiting an elderly neighbour whose cat has recently passed away(n)

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Footage of emergency services personnel assaulted in the line of duty. An arrest in Edinburgh escalates, resulting in an officer severing an artery. Meanwhile, PC Ian Bruce, who is part of a specialist team tackling vehicle crime, finds himself alone and vulnerable after members of a gang he is chasing turn on him(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Aussie comic Adam Hills hosts an evening of stand-up at the Apollo in Hammersmith, London, treating the audience to his own brand of humour before introducing routines by actress, comedienne and shoe-lover Andi Osho and funnyman-turned-EastEnder Terry Alderton(n)

programme.desc: A schoolboy relives the day he was injured while practicing BMX tricks, and a horse rider recalls the moment when she was thrown by her mount in the middle of an amateur race(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich come up with plans for a tranquil garden in Newport, Wales, which must be suitable for growing herbs and practicing meditation(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany berates Bianca for not being there for her and Bobby is stunned when he overhears Rainie and Ian talking about their past. Gray feels increasingly left out by the Taylors and takes it out on Chantelle, while Jean lies about her operation being postponed to stop everyone fussing - but Daniel sees through her lies(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric celebrates his 65th birthday, but as he continues to make mistakes at work, the return of a face from the past forces him to realise that his surgical skills are fading. Fletch risks his chances of making peace with Ange as he tries to discover if Chloe's story is true, and Essie is forced to assume the role of a newly adoptive mother after Sacha jumps to the wrong conclusion(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: The crucial CCTV footage of Shaun's supposed crime is seized by a mysterious MI5 officer, leaving Rachel convinced the truth behind the case is not as clear cut as it first appeared. However, getting answers may prove difficult when the surveillance system is found wanting. After a confrontation with his ex-partner, Shaun is determined to prove his innocence, and recruits an old friend to help him break into Hannah's flat and find evidence. Ron Perlman (Hellboy) joins the cast of the conspiracy thriller(n)

programme.title: Canny Cops
programme.desc: Terry gives Shauna one final warning when the police receive a report of drugs at her house. Along the coast, a brutal bar brawl leaves a man seriously injured - and the pub forced to close. In Dawdon, a man is suspected of stabbing a local resident, but proves difficult to track down after he goes into hiding - exposing deep-seated trust issues between the police and the townsfolk(n)

programme.title: Reggie Yates: Extreme Russia
programme.desc: The broadcaster travels to Siberia with international model scouts in search of new recruits. In the area's capital of Novosibirsk, he explores how girls as young as five are training to join the industry by learning how to walk, pose, diet and apply make up. He also questions how likely the hopefuls are to succeed, investigates the pitfalls of the business and finds out what lives the successful youngsters are leaving behind(n)

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Live from Manchester, Michelle Ackerley finds out how the UK Football Policing Unit keeps fans and the public safe. In the studio, Rav Wilding has the latest appeals and CCTV(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is on a property-searching mission in the Devon countryside, helping a mother and daughter duo with £750,000 find a home for three generations of their family and an eco-camping business. The presenter also visits Chagford Community Farm, one of the UK's first community-supported micro-dairies and sees how residents are readily signing up to buying its local produce(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden for Neil and Jack, two friends living together in Leeds who want a space to entertain their guests(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: With their car boot full of antiques, Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst head to an auction in Hertfordshire, hoping to make a profit on some old biscuit tins(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Comedy panel show, hosted by Rob Brydon, in which team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by celebrity guests Adrian Edmondson, Claude Littner, Cariad Lloyd and Jordan Stephens to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contestants face two more culinary challenges, as they compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. First, the celebrities are split into two teams of two, and each team is tasked with cooking the same dish, which must be identical in both appearance and flavour. For the second challenge, the competitors are then sent out on their first mass catering challenge, taking place at the Ford factory in Dagenham, Essex(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, former The Only Way is Essex star and presenter Mark Wright investigates his family history. First, he examines his father's side of the family, and uncovers surprising facts about his Sephardic Jewish ancestry and his relation to a master swordsman living in late 17th-century Andalucia. Mark's journey then takes him to Spain, where he is astonished to find out how one of his relatives survived the terror of the Spanish Inquisition, and another was burned at the stake(n)

programme.title: Driven: The Billy Monger Story
programme.desc: Documentary following 18-year-old Billy Monger as he attempts to get back into motor racing after being involved in a crash in April 2017 that resulted in the amputation of his legs. With the support of the racing community and his hero Lewis Hamilton, Billy will have to change the rules of motorsport and learn to drive again without legs, all while coming to terms with life as a person with a disability(n)

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace examines how supermarkets are fighting to bring consumers their food on demand, and finds out how Ocado are using robots to revolutionise online delivery. Elsewhere in the programme, he also visits a state of the art lettuce farm in Norfolk which supplies 300 million lettuces a year, and Nikki Fox tests out the Co-op's new pay in the aisle technology as we move towards a checkout-free future(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie and the Rich brothers help Elizabeth from Brighton to turn a paved-over blank square of land into a city garden full of colour and plants, on a healthy budget of £6,000(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antique experts Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson are on the last leg of their coast-to-coast journey from Morecambe to Newcastle(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: RNLI volunteers in Pembrokeshire, including brothers Hal, Will and Ben, race to the scene when a plane makes an emergency crash landing on one of Wales' busiest beaches. In Jersey, the St Catherine crew are pushed to the brink when they search for a man who has gone missing at sea in the middle of a fierce winter storm. On the Wirral, the crew of Hoylake launch their specialist rescue hovercraft and race the incoming tide to try and save a horse, which has become stuck fast in the mud and at risk from drowning(n)

programme.desc: Highlights of the week's proceedings in Parliament(n)

programme.title: The Life and Death of Colonel Blimp (1943)
programme.desc: A Home Guard officer serving during the Second World War is outraged by the disrespect for tradition shown by younger soldiers. He looks back on his military adventures during the Boer War and First World War, as well as his three great loves. Michael Powell and Emeric Pressburger's satirical drama, starring Roger Livesey, Deborah Kerr, Anton Walbrook and John Laurie(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at television and film appearances made over the years by actress Deborah Kerr, capturing the milestones and highlights of her life and career that led to her being considered a Hollywood icon. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from the Grand Pier at Weston-super-Mare in Somerset, where experts Catherine Southon and Jonathan Pratt pick out some gems to value. Among them are rare medals that once belonged to an early aviator and puppets that look like the Swedish band Abba. Plus, Paul delves into the history of seaside entertainment.(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Jeremy Vine in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Born to Be Wild
programme.desc: Head of birds Lorraine and vet Romain join forces when a juvenile raven with a broken wing requires a groundbreaking operation if it is to have a chance of flying again(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four more treasured family heirlooms and the memories they hold back to life, tackling an old Salford tram seat, a vintage horse-racing game, two stained-glass panels, and a mirror box with a theatrical past(n)

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell hosts another first-round match as Rome enthusiasts the Gladiators take on wordsmiths the Lexplorers. The teams must use patience, lateral thinking and sheer inspiration to make connections between four things that may appear at first not to be linked, with one set of clues consisting of Don Quixote, Pandarus, Gargantua and Mrs Malaprop(n)

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The eighth first-round match sees four students from Durham University pit their wits against a team from Trinity College, Cambridge in a bid to reach the second stage. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions(n)

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: It is 1933 and Adolf Hitler is the chancellor of Germany, but he does not have absolute power as there is still a democratic parliament, a head of state and the rule of law. Hitler calls a general election to increase Nazi representation, but a fire in the Reichstag building is blamed on communists - prompting thousands of arrests of left-wingers. Hitler then calls on President von Hindenburg to act and a decree is passed giving the Nazis emergency powers to ban free speech and the right to protest(n)

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Mark Owen reflects on the year his band Take That took the nation by storm and Gladiators became essential Saturday-night viewing, bringing characters such as Wolf and Jet to the screen. Wayne's World's spoof homage to rock was a box-office hit, while violent thriller Reservoir Dogs shot director Quentin Tarantino into the limelight. On the musical front, the Shamen's Ebeneezer Goode brought dance music to the charts _ and caused a storm of controversy over its alleged Ecstasy-promoting lyrics(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: Golf: Walker Cup Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the 47th staging of the amateur team competition between Great Britain & Ireland and USA, held this year at Royal Liverpool Golf Club in Hoylake(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: The rehearsals for Chicago are finally under way, having been delayed by Fosse's hospitalisation for a heart attack, but his insecurities soon come to the fore. Bob is hard on Gwen in rehearsals and suggests she is having problems keeping up with the younger cast, while she is concerned about the ending of the show and asks for a new song(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: As the town is engulfed by a storm, Jac faces a devastating confrontation with her old rival Faye Byrne and the man whom they both loved. Sacha helps a grieving family that is torn over the issue of organ donation to come to a difficult decision. Patsy Kensit reprises her role as Faye for the first time since 2010(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: In a special edition marking 80 years since the first wave of evacuees were sent from towns and cities to the safety of the countryside, Steve Brown retraces his grandmother Rose's journey from Croydon to Cornwall. Sean Fletcher experiences life as an evacuee with schoolchildren at Acton Scott Historic Farm, while Margherita Taylor meets people who were relocated to Chatsworth House for safety during the war(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Sitting side by side on the banks of the Upper Tamar Lakes, Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer fish for the predatory perch. Bob notices that Paul has far more superior seating, which causes momentary tension between the pair. The tables are turned when Bob reveals this week's accommodation - leaving Paul almost speechless. The following day the duo brave the open sea and have a very surprising catch. They celebrate in the only way possible in the south-west of England - it's not particularly heart-healthy but it proves to be very tasty(n)

programme.desc: Families from Mersea Island, Essex, and Morecambe, Lancashire, put their homes and lifestyles under the microscope as they exchange properties. After the swap, they reveal whether or not they enjoyed the experience(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: A tip-off about an empty house prompts a search for Elizabeth Dean's heirs, while Michael Buerk and the team reveal a colonial link to slavery in Henry Arthur Coke's case(n)

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: The construction of HMS Astute, the Royal Navy's biggest and most powerful attack submarine, which cost more than £1billion and is able to circumnavigate the globe without surfacing. The programme follows engineers, designers and skilled craftsmen as they work on the vessel, from the initial stages of production to its first journey into the open sea(n)

programme.title: Shark
programme.desc: A look at how scientists are challenging traditional perceptions of sharks, with breakthroughs regarding the creatures' intelligence and social skills presenting them in a surprising new light. The programme also highlights some researchers' beliefs that studying the predators could help to solve some of the most pressing medical challenges of the 21st century. Narrated by Paul McGann(n)

programme.desc: The team heads to Norwich Cathedral, where Thomas Plant and Kate Bateman unearth items including a religious relic and a Martin Brothers vase, and Paul Martin visits the circus(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz show in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Born to Be Wild
programme.desc: Staff at the SSPCA's wildlife rescue centre take care of two white-tailed eagles and have to use all their experience and expertise to try to get them back in the wild(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The team restores a threadbare theatre chair from Derby Hippodrome, a rare timepiece that has not worked for years, a cherished bronze locket and two First World War flying mascots(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: On the east coast of Scotland, volunteers from Dunbar race to save three children trapped in a cave by rising tides, while in the west, the Tobermory crew comes to the aid of a yacht caught in a storm and being forced onto the rocks. In England, the Blyth crew searches for an 80-year-old man who has gone missing after leaving home to look for lobsters on the coast(n)

programme.title: Seahorse: The Dad Who Gave Birth (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary about a gay transgender man's efforts to have a baby, following his story over the course of three years from the initial struggle to conceive to the eventual birth, and examining the challenges and prejudices he has faced, forcing him to rely on the support of friends and family in the face of mounting hostility(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: NFL This Week
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present action from the opening round of fixtures, including New England Patriots v Pittsburgh Steelers(n)

programme.title: China: A New World Order
programme.desc: The second part of the documentary examines how President Xi Jinping has made China a more assertive presence on the world stage since his accession in 2013. For decades, Beijing followed a policy of 'hide your strength and bide your time', but that has changed - creating alarm in the West over China's growing influence. As Xi faces his greatest test yet - a confrontation in Hong Kong - how will the newly resurgent China react?(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and Philip Mould investigate what could turn out to be one of the best bargains ever seen on the programme. Almost 30 years ago, Bob Kay bought a junk shop still life for £1. If it turns out to be by Giorgio de Chirico, it could be worth up to £50,000. Fiona and Philip visit Rome, where de Chirico lived for most of his life, and while they learn a great deal about the painter's status as a master of 20th-century art, they also discover he is widely faked. However, they do come across some interesting documents with which to begin their investigation(n)

programme.title: One Day That Changed My Life
programme.desc: A former star of West End musical Billy Elliot tries to secure a place at ballet school and a profoundly deaf woman takes her driving test in a bid to secure her dream job(n)

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Kirsten O'Brien takes a 15-mile walk along the River Tee, exploring the history of Upper Teesdale, meeting some of extraordinary people who call its banks home and taking a wild swim in its white waters. She begins her trek at South Gare near Redcar - a childhood playground for Kirsten and her brother - and then takes in High Force, Middleton and ancient Egglestone Abbey(n)

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: The story of Syrian refugee families who have made their homes on the island of Bute in the Firth of Clyde, finding a place of safety after the horrors of the Syrian war they have escaped. Mounzer runs the Orient Salon, named after the busy barber shop he ran in his native Damascus, while next door Rahaf and her husband Fadel run a restaurant and takeaway bringing a taste of Syria to Bute. Morven Christie narrates(n)

programme.desc: Families from Devon and Kent exchange homes and lifestyles in an experience that will put the way they live to the test. After the swap, they reveal whether they enjoyed themselves and what they have learned(n)

programme.title: Heir Hunters
programme.desc: The probate detectives investigate the affairs of a former insurance worker who has died leaving a valuable estate in Battersea, south London. Meanwhile, the case of a bus fanatic reveals the story of how a band of eagle-eyed servicemen defended Britain's shores(n)

programme.title: How to Build
programme.desc: Documentary following workers at Rolls-Royce's Derby factory as they construct the company's Trent 1000 jet engine in preparation for its maiden flight attached to a Boeing 787 Dreamliner. The film explores the technology involved in its construction, and charts the ups and downs of working on the assembly line. Narrated by Gerard Fletcher(n)

programme.title: Tigers About the House
programme.desc: First of a two-part programme catching up with zookeeper Giles Clark and the Sumatran tiger cubs he was hand-rearing in his home in the suburbs of Queensland. Spot and Stripe are approaching their first birthdays and Giles has his hands full guiding them from adolescence into adulthood, while Spot faces uncertainty about his eyesight and his relationship with Stripe. The Clarks also make a big decision to safeguard the cub's wild cousins, with Giles, his wife Kerri and son Kynan embarking on a road trip through Indonesia with the aim of helping to protect endangered species(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At Muncaster Castle in Cumbria, experts Thomas Plant and Catherine Southon choose items for auction, including a collection of First World War memorabilia and an array of postcards(n)

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize(n)

programme.title: Born to Be Wild
programme.desc: The team trial a ground-breaking new technique as they help rehabilitate a pair of orphaned seals, and have their hands full dealing with a pair of orphaned barn owl chicks(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests with interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest news on Brexit(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Restorers get to work on a grandfather clock, hoping to make it chime again, and doctors perform surgery on a teddy bear with only one ear and one arm after a run-in with a puppy(n)

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: The five remaining contestants take part in a commercial design challenge to transform holiday lodges in the Lincolnshire woods. Split into a team of two and a team of three, the competitors embark on the biggest project of the series so far, designing a bedroom each, along with a shared kitchen-diner and an outdoor area. Presented by Fearne Cotton(n)

programme.title: The Big Hospital Experiment
programme.desc: In a radical social experiment based on a scheme in Germany, 14 young volunteers between the ages of 18 and 24 spend four weeks delivering patient care on the NHS frontline at Royal Derby Hospital, aiming to find out whether volunteers could help relieve the pressures of staff on the wards and make a positive difference to patient care in the NHS. This time, the volunteers embark on their second week at Royal Derby Hospital, where some participants struggle to deal with the difficult realities of life and death. Michael and Piotr work alongside the emergency team to assist an elderly patient who has fallen in her garden, while Alishba offers support to the mother of a young boy who has been brought in for an MRI(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Taking the children to visit her Aunt and Uncle in Vancouver, Sam learns an upsetting family secret. Starring Pamela Adlon, Olivia Edward, Hannah Alligood and Mikey Madison. Written by Pamela Adlon and Louis CK(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass re-investigate the case of Edward Devlin and Alfred Burns, who were arrested and sentenced to death for the 1951 murder of Liverpudlian Beatrice Rimmer(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom's Year with the Birds
programme.desc: As spring makes way for summer in Exmoor, wildlife expert Johnny Kingdom is relieved to discover a healthy brood of wren chicks and finds that some of the adults survived the harsh winter. He is also pleased with his footage of young swallows, and returns to one of his old hides in a bid to film the offspring of a pair of barn owls, as well as enlisting the help of an old friend to record the behaviour of woodpecker chicks(n)

programme.title: Rome: A History of the Eternal City
programme.desc: Historian Simon Sebag Montefiore reveals how Rome cast aside its pantheon of pagan gods, and Emperor Constantine took a huge leap of faith by promoting Christianity as the religion of the empire. He charts the rocky course of Rome's rise to become the capital of Western Christendom, and its impact on the lives of its citizens, elites and high priests(n)

programme.title: Great Indian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Jodhpur to Delhi, heading into the desert to take a camel ride to a village where life has changed little in centuries, before embarking on the Jaipur-Agra-Delhi 'Golden Triangle' tour - India's must-do itinerary in 1913 as today. Michael also visits Agra, where he learns how the Taj Mahal, built by a Mughal emperor, was restored by a British viceroy at the time of his Bradshaw's guide book(n)

programme.title: A Timewatch Guide
programme.desc: Saul David uses the BBC archive to chart the history of the world's most destructive war, looking at how the story of the conflict has changed as new information comes to light and forgotten stories are remembered. He examines the most important accounts of the Second World War, and how the understanding of them has been redefined since the conflict ended(n)

programme.title: Fighting for King and Empire: Britain's Caribbean Heroes
programme.desc: During the Second World War, thousands of men and women from Britain's Caribbean colonies volunteered to join the fight against Hitler, but their contribution has largely been forgotten. In this documentary based on the film Divided By Race - United in War and Peace, veterans tell their extraordinary wartime stories and reveal how they have faced a lifelong struggle to be treated as equals by the UK government and the British people(n)

programme.title: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: Bettany Hughes' series profiling the most celebrated thinkers of the Ancient world continues as she turns her attention to Socrates. Heading to Greece, she details how the Athenian philosopher secured a reputation as an influential maverick. She also highlights how his contributions as one of the founders of Western philosophy did not please his detractors, as his outspoken defence of his beliefs ultimately led to his execution(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: The history of the coach, which offered a cheaper alternative to rail travel upon its arrival in the 1950s and gave greater freedom to people previously restricted to their own villages and towns due to limited funds. The documentary looks at the distinctive liveries employed by different transport companies and the activities of passengers, including singing and pub visits, as they travelled to destinations such as Blackpool, Margate and Torquay. Narrated by June Whitfield(n)

programme.title: Metalworks!
programme.desc: Tobias Capwell explores how Henry VIII combined German technology with Renaissance artistry in his quest to conform to the image of the perfect knight. He also reveals how the monarch's daughter, Elizabeth I, exploited the image further by making her courtiers parade before her in the most innovative and richly decorated suits of armour ever commissioned in steel(n)

programme.title: Rome: A History of the Eternal City
programme.desc: Historian Simon Sebag Montefiore reveals how Rome cast aside its pantheon of pagan gods, and Emperor Constantine took a huge leap of faith by promoting Christianity as the religion of the empire. He charts the rocky course of Rome's rise to become the capital of Western Christendom, and its impact on the lives of its citizens, elites and high priests(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Great Irish Journeys with Martha Kearney
programme.desc: The broadcaster follows in the footsteps of 19th-century artist George Victor Du Noyer, who spent 35 years charting the landscape, people and buildings of Ireland. In the first episode, Martha gives an introduction to Du Noyer's work, and travels to the Bog of Allen, which lies between the Liffey and Shannon, and the Old Head of Kinsale in Co Cork(n)

programme.title: Beyond the Walls: In Search of the Celts
programme.desc: Historian Dr Eleanor Barraclough travels through some of Britain's most beautiful landscapes - Hadrian's Wall, the Lake District and Offa's Dyke - in search of new evidence to reveal the true story of the mysterious ancient British tribes often called the Celts(n)

programme.title: The Troubles: A Secret History
programme.desc: Documentary unearthing new evidence on the beginnings of the Troubles. The first episode focuses on unrest between unionists and nationalists in the 1960s and the arrival of the British army towards the decade's end, and the downfall of the Northern Ireland government in 1972, when IRA members believed they were about to force the British Army out of Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Squaddies on the Frontline
programme.desc: The story of the British Army's experience of the Northern Ireland conflict between 1969 and 2007, told through the eyes of the men and women that were stationed there, who were at the heart of the key events throughout the conflict(n)

programme.title: Steve Jobs: Billion Dollar Hippy
programme.desc: Evan Davis traces the history of Steve Jobs' time in control at Apple, with contributions from insiders including the company's co-founder Steve Wozniak, world wide web inventor Tim Berners-Lee, branding guru Rita Clifton and Stephen Fry. The presenter hears stories of how Jobs, a college dropout, was involved in the company's rise from operating out of a suburban garage to becoming a global brand(n)

programme.title: Rebel Physician: Nicholas Culpeper's Fight for Medical Freedom
programme.desc: Profile of the noted botanist, who translated Latin medicine books into English and researched the healing properties of plants. He died of tuberculosis in 1654, aged 37. Presented by Benjamin Woolley(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Timeshift documentary exploring Britons' love of coach journeys in recent years, from school trips to outings for football fans and band tours. The programme looks at efforts to maintain interest in the mode of transport, including alternatives to the National Coach Company's standardised offerings, introduced in the 1970s. Narrated by Julian Rhind-Tutt(n)

programme.title: How Quizzing Got Cool: TV's Brains of Britain
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell, Judith Keppel, Chris Tarrant, Mark Labbett and Nicholas Parsons help Ben Miller trace the origins of the pro-quizzers; quiz show veterans who have emerged from the crowd of quiz aficionados in recent years to achieve fame and fortune for their general knowledge prowess(n)

programme.title: Smile! The Nation's Family Album
programme.desc: A revealing portrait that documents the changes in digital technology and how we capture images in the modern day, looking back across generations and the way that families collected their sentimental snapshots and shared experiences, offering an intimate portrait of Britain's post-war social history(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Great Irish Journeys with Martha Kearney
programme.desc: The broadcaster continues to trace the footsteps of 19th-century artist and geologist George Victor Du Noyer, who spent 35 years charting the landscape, people and buildings of Ireland. Martha travels north, taking in the extraordinary beauty of Dunluce Castle, and onwards to Mussenden Temple, pondering its unusual location. She also marvels at how Belfast was shaped by the Victorians, visits Kearney Point on the Ards Peninsula and journeys south to Dunmoe, which overlooks the site of the Battle of the Boyne(n)

programme.title: Life of a Mountain: A Year on Blencathra
programme.desc: Award-winning film-maker Terry Abraham returns to the Lake District to showcase 'the people's mountain' - Blencathra, looking at the lives of local residents, schoolchildren and tourists. Featuring contributions from comedian Ed Byrne, broadcaster Stuart Maconie, mountaineer Alan Hinkes OBE and record-breaking fell runner Steve Birkinshaw(n)

programme.title: Raiders of the Lost Past with Janina Ramirez
programme.desc: Dr Janina Ramirez looks back at the discovery of three extraordinary pre-war treasures, and the explorer-archaeologists who came across them. This time, she examines the discovery of the 40,000-year-old artwork known as The Lion Man, found in a remote cave in Germany in 1939 by Nazi party member and archaeologist, Robert Wetzel(n)

programme.title: Vikings
programme.desc: Neil Oliver explores the history of the Norse warriors, delving into their customs and searching for the truth behind their fearsome reputation. He begins by telling the story of their origins, and investigates the world of their ancient ancestors. The programme charts Scandinavia's prehistoric landscape, from the Baltic Sea to Norway's Atlantic coast, and Neil examines archaeological evidence to create a portrait of the society from which the Vikings emerged(n)

programme.title: Hidden Killers
programme.desc: Suzannah Lipscomb explores the hidden dangers of properties built in the Tudor period by looking at the new style of multi-room homes that only the aristocracy had previously been able to afford, which were now being constructed by the emerging wealthy classes. Plus, the presenter looks at the exotic new goods brought in from the New World by merchants that could actually be deadly(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Growing numbers of young people are carrying knives and becoming victims of knife crime, while doctors report that the injuries from knives they are treating in hospital are becoming more severe and the victims getting younger. In this programme, Chanell Wallace, whose own brother was stabbed to death, meets young people growing up in communities where carrying a knife is normal, sees how violence from knife crime is turning lives upside down and spends time in a school and college to see the impact of knives on the classroom(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: This second heat week sees five more celebrities enter the MasterChef kitchen. with Dom Parker, Josie Long, Mim Shaikh, Jenny Ryan and Alex George facing three culinary challenges. They first tackle an invention test, before they are split into two groups to take on the challenge of cooking for the first time in a restaurant kitchen, preparing dishes for paying customers. In the final task they are asked to prepare a dish of their own design in just one hour, before judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace decide which of the contestants will leave the competition(n)

programme.title: This Country

programme.title: Critical Incident

programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich come up with plans for a tranquil garden in Newport, Wales, which must be suitable for growing herbs and practicing meditation(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Tiffany berates Bianca for not being there for her and Bobby is stunned when he overhears Rainie and Ian talking about their past. Gray feels increasingly left out by the Taylors and takes it out on Chantelle, while Jean lies about her operation being postponed to stop everyone fussing - but Daniel sees through her lies(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric celebrates his 65th birthday, but as he continues to make mistakes at work, the return of a face from the past forces him to realise that his surgical skills are fading. Fletch risks his chances of making peace with Ange as he tries to discover if Chloe's story is true, and Essie is forced to assume the role of a newly adoptive mother after Sacha jumps to the wrong conclusion(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: The crucial CCTV footage of Shaun's supposed crime is seized by a mysterious MI5 officer, leaving Rachel convinced the truth behind the case is not as clear cut as it first appeared. However, getting answers may prove difficult when the surveillance system is found wanting. After a confrontation with his ex-partner, Shaun is determined to prove his innocence, and recruits an old friend to help him break into Hannah's flat and find evidence. Ron Perlman (Hellboy) joins the cast of the conspiracy thriller(n)

programme.title: Canny Cops
programme.desc: Terry gives Shauna one final warning when the police receive a report of drugs at her house. Along the coast, a brutal bar brawl leaves a man seriously injured - and the pub forced to close. In Dawdon, a man is suspected of stabbing a local resident, but proves difficult to track down after he goes into hiding - exposing deep-seated trust issues between the police and the townsfolk(n)

programme.title: Reggie Yates: Extreme Russia
programme.desc: The broadcaster travels to Siberia with international model scouts in search of new recruits. In the area's capital of Novosibirsk, he explores how girls as young as five are training to join the industry by learning how to walk, pose, diet and apply make up. He also questions how likely the hopefuls are to succeed, investigates the pitfalls of the business and finds out what lives the successful youngsters are leaving behind(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The development progress of a semi-detached house in Willenhall near Wolverhampton, a particularly colourful property in Liverpool and an overgrown plot of land in west London(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Trading Standards Officers investigate a travel company boss conning customers, while officers examine a sewage leak which polluted a stream running through an Oxfordshire village(n)

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.desc: Emma has to deal with an awkward situation involving a patient, Karen hears some gossip, and Ruhma finds herself caught up at the hospital(n)

programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is on a property-searching mission in the Devon countryside, helping a mother and daughter duo with £750,000 find a home for three generations of their family and an eco-camping business. The presenter also visits Chagford Community Farm, one of the UK's first community-supported micro-dairies and sees how residents are readily signing up to buying its local produce(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich transform a garden for Neil and Jack, two friends living together in Leeds who want a space to entertain their guests(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: With their car boot full of antiques, Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst head to an auction in Hertfordshire, hoping to make a profit on some old biscuit tins(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio(n)

programme.title: The Welsh Italians
programme.desc: Michela Chiappa concludes her documentary with a visit to the Italian town of Bardi, where her family and many other Welsh-Italians came from. The place is buzzing with Welsh people visiting for a week-long festival, culminating in a street party to celebrate the immigrants who return each year(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contestants face two more culinary challenges, as they compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. First, the celebrities are split into two teams of two, and each team is tasked with cooking the same dish, which must be identical in both appearance and flavour. For the second challenge, the competitors are then sent out on their first mass catering challenge, taking place at the Ford factory in Dagenham, Essex(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, former The Only Way is Essex star and presenter Mark Wright investigates his family history. First, he examines his father's side of the family, and uncovers surprising facts about his Sephardic Jewish ancestry and his relation to a master swordsman living in late 17th-century Andalucia. Mark's journey then takes him to Spain, where he is astonished to find out how one of his relatives survived the terror of the Spanish Inquisition, and another was burned at the stake(n)

programme.title: Driven: The Billy Monger Story
programme.desc: Documentary following 18-year-old Billy Monger as he attempts to get back into motor racing after being involved in a crash in April 2017 that resulted in the amputation of his legs. With the support of the racing community and his hero Lewis Hamilton, Billy will have to change the rules of motorsport and learn to drive again without legs, all while coming to terms with life as a person with a disability(n)

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace examines how supermarkets are fighting to bring consumers their food on demand, and finds out how Ocado are using robots to revolutionise online delivery. Elsewhere in the programme, he also visits a state of the art lettuce farm in Norfolk which supplies 300 million lettuces a year, and Nikki Fox tests out the Co-op's new pay in the aisle technology as we move towards a checkout-free future(n)

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Dougie's mental state unravels after he finds about about Scarlett's lies, while Joe tries to find a way out of the corner he has been backed into, and Nicole must try anything to keep Alex's interest(n)

programme.title: The Capture
programme.desc: The crucial CCTV footage of Shaun's supposed crime is seized by a mysterious MI5 officer, leaving Rachel convinced the truth behind the case is not as clear cut as it first appeared. However, getting answers may prove difficult when the surveillance system is found wanting. After a confrontation with his ex-partner, Shaun is determined to prove his innocence, and recruits an old friend to help him break into Hannah's flat and find evidence. Ron Perlman (Hellboy) joins the cast of the conspiracy thriller(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Ric celebrates his 65th birthday, but as he continues to make mistakes at work, the return of a face from the past forces him to realise that his surgical skills are fading. Fletch risks his chances of making peace with Ange as he tries to discover if Chloe's story is true, and Essie is forced to assume the role of a newly adoptive mother after Sacha jumps to the wrong conclusion(n)

programme.title: Canny Cops
programme.desc: Terry gives Shauna one final warning when the police receive a report of drugs at her house. Along the coast, a brutal bar brawl leaves a man seriously injured - and the pub forced to close. In Dawdon, a man is suspected of stabbing a local resident, but proves difficult to track down after he goes into hiding - exposing deep-seated trust issues between the police and the townsfolk(n)

programme.title: Reggie Yates: Extreme Russia
programme.desc: The broadcaster travels to Siberia with international model scouts in search of new recruits. In the area's capital of Novosibirsk, he explores how girls as young as five are training to join the industry by learning how to walk, pose, diet and apply make up. He also questions how likely the hopefuls are to succeed, investigates the pitfalls of the business and finds out what lives the successful youngsters are leaving behind(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: The four remaining contestants face two more culinary challenges, as they compete to secure a place in the semi-finals. First, the celebrities are split into two teams of two, and each team is tasked with cooking the same dish, which must be identical in both appearance and flavour. For the second challenge, the competitors are then sent out on their first mass catering challenge, taking place at the Ford factory in Dagenham, Essex(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, former The Only Way is Essex star and presenter Mark Wright investigates his family history. First, he examines his father's side of the family, and uncovers surprising facts about his Sephardic Jewish ancestry and his relation to a master swordsman living in late 17th-century Andalucia. Mark's journey then takes him to Spain, where he is astonished to find out how one of his relatives survived the terror of the Spanish Inquisition, and another was burned at the stake(n)

programme.title: Driven: The Billy Monger Story
programme.desc: Documentary following 18-year-old Billy Monger as he attempts to get back into motor racing after being involved in a crash in April 2017 that resulted in the amputation of his legs. With the support of the racing community and his hero Lewis Hamilton, Billy will have to change the rules of motorsport and learn to drive again without legs, all while coming to terms with life as a person with a disability(n)

programme.title: Supermarket Secrets
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace examines how supermarkets are fighting to bring consumers their food on demand, and finds out how Ocado are using robots to revolutionise online delivery. Elsewhere in the programme, he also visits a state of the art lettuce farm in Norfolk which supplies 300 million lettuces a year, and Nikki Fox tests out the Co-op's new pay in the aisle technology as we move towards a checkout-free future(n)

programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits houses in Stourbridge and Liverpool, and there is a look at a Grade II- listed property in Brentwood, Essex, that Martin Roberts first saw back in 2016(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers check hygiene standards in Oxford takeaways while in Studley, Warwickshire, they crack down on a butcher claiming meat was British when it came from overseas(n)

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: A glider pilot's footage captures the moments after he got stuck in a tree, as well as his rescue, and a DJ dad relives the night a car failed to stop at a notorious crossroads(n)

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: There is fallout from Friday night for Becky and Zara, while Theresa and Phil Sutton receive some bad news and Karen suspects one of Sid's patients of something(n)

programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the quiz in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps an expat couple who want to return from Cyprus to buy a house in Lincolnshire, and also visits a college to find out about the next generation of farmers(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich compete as they design gardens for newly married couple Bernard and Cecilia, who want a beautiful space that marries their British and Maltese lives. As well as having hints of both Mediterranean planting and a classic English country garden, they also want to be able to grow vegetables and herbs for their kitchen(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Experts Catherine Southon and Tim Medhurst go off on a search for items, setting out from the South Downs and heading towards an auction in Christchurch on the Dorset coast(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Angellica Bell host the first visit of the week to the One Show studio, featuring the usual mix of topical stories and celebrity chat(n)

programme.title: Home Ground
programme.desc: Gavin Andrews learns about the tradition of flax, while reporter Ruth Sanderson meets a local farmer competing in this year's Fermanagh County Show(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: The events of last week continue to hang heavy in Walford. Chantelle passes her work assessment with flying colours but as the family celebrates Gray seems determined to sabotage the event. Tina is sorely tempted when Shirley tells her about a job she is doing for Phil abroad and asks her to come along(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Growing numbers of young people are carrying knives and becoming victims of knife crime, while doctors report that the injuries from knives they are treating in hospital are becoming more severe and the victims getting younger. In this programme, Chanell Wallace, whose own brother was stabbed to death, meets young people growing up in communities where carrying a knife is normal, sees how violence from knife crime is turning lives upside down and spends time in a school and college to see the impact of knives on the classroom(n)

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: This second heat week sees five more celebrities enter the MasterChef kitchen. with Dom Parker, Josie Long, Mim Shaikh, Jenny Ryan and Alex George facing three culinary challenges. They first tackle an invention test, before they are split into two groups to take on the challenge of cooking for the first time in a restaurant kitchen, preparing dishes for paying customers. In the final task they are asked to prepare a dish of their own design in just one hour, before judges John Torode and Gregg Wallace decide which of the contestants will leave the competition(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day from Northern Ireland
programme.desc: Northern Ireland v Germany . Stephen Watson presents action of the Euro 2020 Group C qualifier at Windsor Park. The hosts sat proudly at the top of the table after four matches, but faced their toughest test so far against the Germans. With analysis from Stephen Craigan, and commentary from Thomas Kane(n)

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: The vicar's son returns to the village and has to readjust to rural life having lived in Bristol, while Kerry tries to give back to the community by visiting an elderly neighbour whose cat has recently passed away(n)

programme.title: Critical Incident
programme.desc: Footage of emergency services personnel assaulted in the line of duty. An arrest in Edinburgh escalates, resulting in an officer severing an artery. Meanwhile, PC Ian Bruce, who is part of a specialist team tackling vehicle crime, finds himself alone and vulnerable after members of a gang he is chasing turn on him(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street

programme.desc: The hunt for Sian O'Callaghan continues as Det Supt Steve Fulcher and his team see suspects come and go with no leads to follow. Local woman Karen Edwards notices that she only lives a few doors away from the missing girl's mother, Elaine Pickford, but is only reminded of her own problems, having not seen her daughter Becky for several years. With things looking bleak for Wiltshire police, a brilliant piece of police work finally points Fulcher towards a definite suspect. Fact-based crime drama, starring Martin Freeman(n)

programme.desc: England v Kosovo (Kick-off 7.45pm). Mark Pougatch presents coverage of the Group A qualifier at St Mary's Stadium in Southampton. programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Alan smuggles a woman up to his room, making sure the lights are turned down low so she cannot see how shabby it is - but an unscheduled appearance by her irate father soon puts paid to his dreams of romance. Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale star(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. The family warms to the French way of life, but Charley is unhappy with Mariette's flirting - and after a little Dutch courage, decides to take matters into his own hands. Drama, starring Philip Franks and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Beautiful Russian aristocrat Countess Vera Rossakoff, the chief suspect in a series of jewel thefts, seemingly captivates Poirot with her charm and intellect, meaning Hastings and Miss Lemon have to do most of the actual detective work and ensure she is brought to justice. Drama, guest starring Kika Markham(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Paying guests the Langleys arrive for a shooting party at Ashfordly Hall, but their grandiose ideas upset the locals and it is not long before a sinister plot is revealed. Vernon's latest money-making scheme seems destined to end in disaster as David takes the wheel of an old Army vehicle, but his off-road tours are not for the faint-hearted. Nostalgic rural drama, guest starring Robert Glenister and Celia Imrie(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah is shocked when she sees Jack's black eye, Eric is not keen on setting up a joint account with Elizabeth, and Kim receives an enormous bouquet of flowers(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Lorraine is not pulling her weight at Lynn's, Annie and Sarah fall out over the plan to sell the farm, and Kim tells Neil that Kathy knows everything about their affair(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Audrey cannot wait to see the back of the shop, Angie advises Curly to befriend Elaine, and when Tommy gets the mumps, Vera is worried that Jack will catch it(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The owner of local country magazine Midsomer Life becomes a suspect when a man's body is discovered in woodland, and it is established the victim had been having an affair with his wife. As Barnaby and Jones delve into the case, another murder forces them to reassess their inquiries. Detective drama, starring John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Simon Williams(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Tom and Sybbie return to Downton, but the peace is disrupted by an unexpected visit from an old friend. Baxter is faced with a dilemma after an encounter with Sergeant Willis, while Thomas struggles to fill Carson's shoes. Anna goes to London with Mary to deal with a crisis, while her secrets seem likely to be exposed. Daisy is not happy with Cora, and Mary has an admirer(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica enters the exciting world of modern music, risking life, limb and eardrums to investigate the baffling murder of a petty crook. Detective drama, starring Angela Lansbury, Tony Plana and Jason Bernard(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The doctor heads for London to meet the man leading the selection process for the job he wants, but has to conquer his blood phobia once and for all to secure the prestigious position. Back in Cornwall, Aunt Joan locks a mischievous schoolboy in a chicken coop - and faces the consequences when he falls ill. Drama, guest starring Nicholas Le Prevost and Gwen Taylor, with Martin Clunes and Stephanie Cole(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The new midwife in the village persuades Louisa to take a natural, holistic approach to treating an infection, infuriating Martin, who insists on conventional medicine. Meanwhile, Bert is smitten with the flirtatious new cook working in his restaurant, and the doctor hears good news about the job in London. Gemma Craven guest stars, with Martin Clunes, Caroline Catz and Ian McNeice(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Janet comes face to face with the man who left her fighting for life just months earlier - serial killer Geoff Hastings, who has vowed to finally reveal the full extent of his crimes. Rachel and the rest of the team investigate the racially motivated murder of a taxi driver. On the personal front, Andy refuses to take no for an answer, so Janet threatens to report him to the DCI. Crime drama, starring Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones, with an appearance by Kevin Doyle (Downton Abbey's Molesley)(n)

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Rachel meets Sean's son, who turns out to be an eight-year-old version of his dad, and the idea of becoming a stepmum puts her further off the idea of marriage than she was before - not that she even agreed in the first place. Andy asks Janet out for a drink, but when she turns him down he accuses her of leading him on. A woman in a sexy cop's uniform turns up dead, and the DCI assumes the killer was her lover - the question is, which one of her many lovers was it? And to add to the mystery, why was her face painted green?(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A kidnapper demands a ransom from a supermarket magnate in return for sparing the life of a boy who has been reported missing. Frost manages to identify a likely suspect - but with no clues to go on, it looks as if throwing away the rule book may be the only way to save the child. David Jason and Bruce Alexander star(n)

programme.title: On the Buses

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Alan and Philip goad Rigsby into fasting for three days - and it is not long before he is stealing Vienna's cat food to stave off the pangs of hunger. With Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part one of two. Pop busies himself organising a christening while awaiting the birth of his first grandchild - and still finds time to cast a beady eye over the contents of an elderly widow's home. Whimsical rural drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris, Philip Franks and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A lavish dinner party proves to be a prelude to murder and intrigue - but fortunately Poirot is among the guests, and soon realises the key to the mystery lies in a duel fought 10 years previously. Drama, starring David Suchet, Caroline Langrishe and John McEnery(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A father is aghast to read tales of wild romance in his teenage daughter's diary, failing to realise they are all fictitious. David inherits a ventriloquist's dummy complete with a schedule of bookings, and decides the show must go on. Meanwhile, Gina finds herself attracted to a charismatic guest. Nostalgic drama, starring David Lonsdale and Tricia Penrose(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank has to go to Edinburgh, so Kim plans a day Christmas shopping with Neil. Carol is amazed when Archie offers to buy her a drink, and Annie is taken ill(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: The day of the Home Farm shoot arrives, Annie is in bed with bronchitis, Nick gets the wrong idea about Archie and Julie, and Mark feels terrible after a night in the Woolpack(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve refuses to have Jim in court with him, Alf throws a farewell party at the Rovers, and Don explains to Jack that he only shares the house with Ivy(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Brendan takes over the shop and renames it Best Buys, Vera is horrified when he tells her he inherited her debts, and Jack is enjoying living apart from Vera and her nagging(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A magician's assistant drops dead on stage, poisoned by the toxins from a tropical frog. While Barnaby and Jones begin their investigations, tempers flare in the local community between Ernest, a firm believer in a mystical cult, and his former friend Aloysius - whose background in the jungles of South America makes him the prime suspect for the murder. Starring John Nettles, Ronald Pickup and Stuart Wilson(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Neville Chamberlain visits Downton for dinner, and is drawn into Violet's plan to save the hospital, with distressing consequences for everyone. Andy finds an unexpected confidante while helping Mr Mason move into Yew Tree Farm, Mary is uncertain if she has met her match, Edith meets Bertie Pelham in London, and Spratt comes to Denker's aid. Mrs Hughes struggles to keep Carson happy, while Mary thinks secrets are being kept from her(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Foul play disrupts preparations for a charity concert organised to save Maine Woods from dastardly developers intent on lining their own pockets at the expense of the environment. Jessica infiltrates the high-decibel world of rock music to investigate(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: During a music masterclass led by a renowned pianist, a shy prodigy suffers a panic attack and runs off to a nearby river. To her horror, she witnesses a woman drowning and a baby left on the riverbank. But when Barnaby arrives, there is no sign of the victim - and he is struck by similarities to another mysterious case that happened 20 years ago at the same spot. Guest starring veteran actor James Fox and Coronation Street's Ian Puleston-Davies (Owen Armstrong), alongside former Corrie star Richard Fleeshman (Craig Harris)(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. A professional hitman bursts into the luxurious family home of accountant Keith Rothwell and shoots him in cold blood. Banks is instantly struck by the strange reactions of the victim's family and evidence comes to light that Rothwell has been living a secret double life. Now investigating the murder of a victim with two identities, Banks and Morton discover a possible link to a major money-laundering operation and are plunged into a high-profile fraud investigation with political ramifications(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. With Pamela Jefferies in a coma following her vicious attack, Banks becomes more determined to find Rothwell's killer. Convinced a vital clue is staring him in the face, he resolves to learn more about Keith Rothwell and, against orders, questions MP Martin Fleming, putting him on a collision course with rival DCI Burgess. Meanwhile, the return of Tom Rothwell to the UK presents Helen with another suspect and an opportunity to put pressure on his family. With Patrick Baladi(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Japp and Poirot investigate the murder of an actress's estranged husband, only to find in the theatrical world, where deception and play-acting are a way of life, nothing is quite what it seems. In time-honoured fashion, it initially looks as though the butler did it - but subsequent inquiries throw up a veritable rogues' gallery of equally likely suspects. Philip Jackson, David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack decide to get fit, disturbed to learn that bus crews have a short life expectancy. Comedy, with Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Festive episode of the 1970s sitcom about a chirpy driver and his conductor pal who see it as their duty to run rings around the long-suffering inspector of the Luxton Bus Company. Stan and Jack look forward to a welcome seasonal break, but on Christmas Eve a workmate phones in sick and the petulant pair are rostered to provide cover for him(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby challenges Philip to a boxing match, convinced Miss Jones will throw herself at his feet if he wins. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington star(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. Preparations get under way for the christening, while Primrose loses her heart to a very confused vicar. Pop gets a black eye, Edith Pilchester tackles a gang of roving Teddy boys and Mademoiselle Dupont begins to feel ill. Drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective looks forward to a quiet Christmas, but is disturbed by the Foreign Office when an Egyptian prince loses a famous ruby - after making the mistake of lending it to a mysterious woman. Guest starring Stephanie Cole, Nigel Le Vaillant and Tariq Alibai(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Gina is suspected of being a hit-and-run driver, while Vernon is hired to escort two singers to a gig - but soon discovers he has an aversion to loud music and offloads the responsibility onto David, who is no match for the conniving pair(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel brings Jayesh home to meet the Sugdens, Neil pressures Kim to come away with him, Michael sneaks out of the house and breaks his curfew time, and Mark resigns from the farm(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: The day of Michael's committal hearing arrives, and he asks Elizabeth not to go with him. Sangeeta is startled by a prowler, and Jack's anger about the farm boils over(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack worries about his debt, Brendan puts all his prices up and Ken's rent, and Curly worries when he discovers Elaine knows all the board members personally(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly is certain Elaine has got the job and worries about his future. Jim hears that his mother has had a heart attack and makes plans to visit her(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is invited to attend a masquerade ball dressed as someone famous and modestly goes as himself - and finds time amid the revelry to solve the murder of aristocratic fellow guest Lord Cronshaw. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The house is made open to the public for a day to raise money for the hospital, an act that divides opinions among the family and the servants. Tensions between Violet and Cora come to a head as a decision is made about the hospital takeover, Mr Dawes has a proposition for Molesley, and Baxter is thrown into turmoil by an unexpected letter. Carson is not happy with Thomas, but all is not as it appears, while Mary and Edith hope to overcome their past and find new happiness with the men in their lives(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The Crawleys watch Henry compete in a motor racing event, an occasion that leaves Mary reeling. Violet hatches a plan which surprises the family and delights Miss Denker, and learns more about Amelia's intentions in befriending Isobel. Molesley and Daisy rise to the challenges of examination day, Andy's secret is brought to light, Thomas feels isolated, and Mrs Patmore and Mrs Hughes teach Carson a lesson(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica examines a recent murder which the police are reluctant to investigate, and discovers a link between the killing and an abduction 11 years previously. Angela Lansbury stars(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist's wife is murdered during an excavation in the Middle East. Fortunately, a vacationing Poirot - who shares with most great detectives an inability to go on holiday without stumbling over a body or two - is on hand to investigate. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Ron Berglas, Georgina Sowerby and Iain Mitchell(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Cassie turns her attention to Brentford house and the theory that one or more of the suspects may have been abused at the gatherings there. Marion is questioned again after Sinead's allegation that David could have been an IRA target. The detectives also look further into the accusations made against Colin while he was working in the city - but something does not quite seem to add up. Was his alleged victim telling the truth - and if not, why would she lie? Cold-case crime drama, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: With the suspects in the murder of David Walker all having either a motive, opportunity or connection to the victim, Cassie and Sunny are left with a massive puzzle to solve. Cassie soon focuses in on Sara, whose alibi seems too perfect, as if it has been put together by an accomplice of the ex-prostitute - and the team is soon investigating the possibility of a wider revenge plan. Meanwhile, interviews with Colin and Marion's loved ones prove revealing. Cold case crime drama, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar, with Badria Timimi, Mark Bonnar and Rosie Cavaliero(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Elizabeth suggests to Eric that they start a joint account, Frank sees Kim and Neil together, and Kathy suspects Kim is having an affair(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Reg is convinced he is going to be fired, Ken is horrified when Deirdre tells him Tracy is not returning to school, and Emily is puzzled by Percy's attitude(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily's story appears in the Gazette, the Duckworths worry that Alf will ask them to repay him all they owe, and the CID warn Mike that they suspect him of fraud(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: The bobbies of Aidensfield try to get to the bottom of a series of burglaries in which the culprit only steals certain valuable objects, including Tricia's postcard collection. Meanwhile, Blaketon attempts to raise support for his local election campaign, with surprising results. Rural police drama, starring Jason Durr, Clare Calbraith and Derek Fowlds(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica tackles a case in Osaka, Japan, where a motorcyclist romancing the daughter of a traditionally minded family finds his life on the line. Angela Lansbury stars(n)

programme.desc: Neville Chamberlain visits Downton for dinner, and is drawn into Violet's plan to save the hospital, with distressing consequences for everyone. Andy finds an unexpected confidante while helping Mr Mason move into Yew Tree Farm, Mary is uncertain if she has met her match, Edith meets Bertie Pelham in London, and Spratt comes to Denker's aid. Mrs Hughes struggles to keep Carson happy, while Mary thinks secrets are being kept from her(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Foul play disrupts preparations for a charity concert organised to save Maine Woods from dastardly developers intent on lining their own pockets at the expense of the environment. Jessica infiltrates the high-decibel world of rock music to investigate(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: During a music masterclass led by a renowned pianist, a shy prodigy suffers a panic attack and runs off to a nearby river. To her horror, she witnesses a woman drowning and a baby left on the riverbank. But when Barnaby arrives, there is no sign of the victim - and he is struck by similarities to another mysterious case that happened 20 years ago at the same spot. Guest starring veteran actor James Fox and Coronation Street's Ian Puleston-Davies (Owen Armstrong), alongside former Corrie star Richard Fleeshman (Craig Harris)(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. A professional hitman bursts into the luxurious family home of accountant Keith Rothwell and shoots him in cold blood. Banks is instantly struck by the strange reactions of the victim's family and evidence comes to light that Rothwell has been living a secret double life. Now investigating the murder of a victim with two identities, Banks and Morton discover a possible link to a major money-laundering operation and are plunged into a high-profile fraud investigation with political ramifications(n)

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. With Pamela Jefferies in a coma following her vicious attack, Banks becomes more determined to find Rothwell's killer. Convinced a vital clue is staring him in the face, he resolves to learn more about Keith Rothwell and, against orders, questions MP Martin Fleming, putting him on a collision course with rival DCI Burgess. Meanwhile, the return of Tom Rothwell to the UK presents Helen with another suspect and an opportunity to put pressure on his family. With Patrick Baladi(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Japp and Poirot investigate the murder of an actress's estranged husband, only to find in the theatrical world, where deception and play-acting are a way of life, nothing is quite what it seems. In time-honoured fashion, it initially looks as though the butler did it - but subsequent inquiries throw up a veritable rogues' gallery of equally likely suspects. Philip Jackson, David Suchet and Hugh Fraser star(n)

programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve refuses to have Jim in court with him, Alf throws a farewell party at the Rovers, and Don explains to Jack that he only shares the house with Ivy(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Brendan takes over the shop and renames it Best Buys, Vera is horrified when he tells her he inherited her debts, and Jack is enjoying living apart from Vera and her nagging(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A father is aghast to read tales of wild romance in his teenage daughter's diary, failing to realise they are all fictitious. David inherits a ventriloquist's dummy complete with a schedule of bookings, and decides the show must go on. Meanwhile, Gina finds herself attracted to a charismatic guest. Nostalgic drama, starring David Lonsdale and Tricia Penrose(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
