programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack decide to get fit, disturbed to learn that bus crews have a short life expectancy. Comedy, with Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Festive episode of the 1970s sitcom about a chirpy driver and his conductor pal who see it as their duty to run rings around the long-suffering inspector of the Luxton Bus Company. Stan and Jack look forward to a welcome seasonal break, but on Christmas Eve a workmate phones in sick and the petulant pair are rostered to provide cover for him(n)

programme.title: Rising Damp
programme.desc: Rigsby challenges Philip to a boxing match, convinced Miss Jones will throw herself at his feet if he wins. Leonard Rossiter, Frances de la Tour, Richard Beckinsale and Don Warrington star(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. Preparations get under way for the christening, while Primrose loses her heart to a very confused vicar. Pop gets a black eye, Edith Pilchester tackles a gang of roving Teddy boys and Mademoiselle Dupont begins to feel ill. Drama, starring David Jason, Pam Ferris and Catherine Zeta-Jones(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective looks forward to a quiet Christmas, but is disturbed by the Foreign Office when an Egyptian prince loses a famous ruby - after making the mistake of lending it to a mysterious woman. Guest starring Stephanie Cole, Nigel Le Vaillant and Tariq Alibai(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Gina is suspected of being a hit-and-run driver, while Vernon is hired to escort two singers to a gig - but soon discovers he has an aversion to loud music and offloads the responsibility onto David, who is no match for the conniving pair(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Rachel brings Jayesh home to meet the Sugdens, Neil pressures Kim to come away with him, Michael sneaks out of the house and breaks his curfew time, and Mark resigns from the farm(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: The day of Michael's committal hearing arrives, and he asks Elizabeth not to go with him. Sangeeta is startled by a prowler, and Jack's anger about the farm boils over(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack worries about his debt, Brendan puts all his prices up and Ken's rent, and Curly worries when he discovers Elaine knows all the board members personally(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Curly is certain Elaine has got the job and worries about his future. Jim hears that his mother has had a heart attack and makes plans to visit her(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is invited to attend a masquerade ball dressed as someone famous and modestly goes as himself - and finds time amid the revelry to solve the murder of aristocratic fellow guest Lord Cronshaw. David Suchet stars(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The house is made open to the public for a day to raise money for the hospital, an act that divides opinions among the family and the servants. Tensions between Violet and Cora come to a head as a decision is made about the hospital takeover, Mr Dawes has a proposition for Molesley, and Baxter is thrown into turmoil by an unexpected letter. Carson is not happy with Thomas, but all is not as it appears, while Mary and Edith hope to overcome their past and find new happiness with the men in their lives(n)

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The Crawleys watch Henry compete in a motor racing event, an occasion that leaves Mary reeling. Violet hatches a plan which surprises the family and delights Miss Denker, and learns more about Amelia's intentions in befriending Isobel. Molesley and Daisy rise to the challenges of examination day, Andy's secret is brought to light, Thomas feels isolated, and Mrs Patmore and Mrs Hughes teach Carson a lesson(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica examines a recent murder which the police are reluctant to investigate, and discovers a link between the killing and an abduction 11 years previously. Angela Lansbury stars(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An archaeologist's wife is murdered during an excavation in the Middle East. Fortunately, a vacationing Poirot - who shares with most great detectives an inability to go on holiday without stumbling over a body or two - is on hand to investigate. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Ron Berglas, Georgina Sowerby and Iain Mitchell(n)

programme.title: Unforgotten
programme.desc: Cassie turns her attention to Brentford house and the theory that one or more of the suspects may have been abused at the gatherings there. Marion is questioned again after Sinead's allegation that David could have been an IRA target. The detectives also look further into the accusations made against Colin while he was working in the city - but something does not quite seem to add up. Was his alleged victim telling the truth - and if not, why would she lie? Cold-case crime drama, starring Nicola Walker and Sanjeev Bhaskar(n)

programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. What the participants do with the money when they find it is up to them, but if they leave early, they leave with nothing. In just the clothes on their back, with a day's supply of water and a few basic tools, the group is abandoned on the treacherous south east side of the island. They are immediately confronted with their first big decision when a helicopter flies overhead and parachutes a box of cash on the north shore. Do they chase the money or try to make camp? Bear keeps a watchful eye on proceedings(n)

programme.title: The Missing Evidence
programme.desc: Documentary Investigating some of the world's most notorious conspiracy theories. The release of the official conclusions as to why the World Trade Centre's twin towers collapsed after the 9/11 attacks in 2001 left many unsatisfied, with video footage of molten metal pouring from the damaged floors and eyewitness reports of explosions not addressed fully and leading to talk of conspiracies. This programme looks at the theory of chemist Frank Greening and metallurgist Christian Simensen, who believe that the 30 tons of aluminum alloys from the two planes themselves are behind these unexplained occurrences(n)

programme.title: World's Greatest Warships
programme.desc: Charting the history of the warship from the dawn of the 20th century to the present day, drawing on expert interviews, footage and computer-generated animations. Later, the Russians send warships to face-off against HMS Duncan - and threaten to fire on them if they launch missiles into Syria. It is a tense affair, and as the orders to fire are given and the strikes on Syria begin, the crew members are at action stations(n)

Seafaring adventure, with Fred MacMurray, Vera Ralston and Robert Douglas(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo

Galapagos tortoise Charlie is losing weight and keepers believe that it's down to bullying from her older sister, so they decide to step in with an innovative system to try and alleviate the problem(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick operates on a five-month-old rabbit with a badly broken hind leg and diagnoses that a much-loved cocker spaniel needs major spinal surgery. The 'Bionic Vet' also meets young couple David and Khaya, who have brought in a three-legged dog that they adopted from Thailand after it was attacked with a machete. Noel knows he must come up with a radical treatment for the animal's remaining front paw, which is injured, and gains inspiration from X-Men's Wolverine for an innovative solution(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick prepares to operate on Jasper, a 17-week-old Staffie puppy whose legs are growing in the wrong direction, while Labrador Mojo has a spinal problem that will lead to paralysis unless he goes into surgery. Plus, when a cat called Lolli, belonging to nine-year-old Lola, is brought in with a broken pelvis, the 'Bionic Vet' must carry out a pioneering procedure to help the animal walk again(n)

programme.title: Live World Para Swimming Championships
programme.desc: Further coverage of day one from the London Aquatic Centre, which doubles as a key qualification event for next year's Tokyo Paralympic Games, with Ellie Simmonds. Alice Tai, Thomas Hamer and Ellie Robinson among the British medal hopes(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Billy and Gwendoline want to hire their chateau out for weddings, but things are not going to plan, while Clive and Tanith hold an open day at their hotel to win over the locals(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Beautiful Railway
programme.desc: The Jacobite steam train crosses the Glenfinnan Viaduct, while the next generation of guide dog puppies is being trained at Waverley station(n)

programme.title: Coastal Railways with Julie Walters
programme.desc: The actress travels on the famous Great Western Railway to Cornwall. She recalls childhood holidays when she stops at Torquay before heading to a supposedly haunted castle at Kingswear. She also uncovers tales of smuggling and joins members of the RNLI on a practice run before visiting an unusual cake shop in Penzance(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Medics at King's College Hospital treat 79-year-old Arthur, who has been brought into resus with acute stomach discomfort and has several underlying conditions that could be to blame. Meanwhile, 20-year-old wine merchant Tom is rushed into A&E after collapsing with sharp chest pains and breathing difficulties, and retired soldier Rockey is concerned about his blurred vision(n)

programme.title: Shocking Emergency Calls
programme.desc: A disturbing 911 call is made in Gainesville, Florida by Don Spirit, who admits to shooting dead his daughter Sarah and her six young children(n)

programme.desc: Tina Watkins has long struggled to get on the housing ladder in the UK. Now, she has decided to make the most of the incredibly good value property in Spain's Granada province and buy her first-ever home. With her best friend Jamie along to help, Jasmine Harman shows Tina just how far her £19,000 budget will stretch(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: The zebras have a newcomer to their herd, Okoth, who starts to make a play for power, which does not go down well with zebra Florence. Meanwhile, Keeper Isolde comes up with an unusual plan to give the zoo's three Komodo dragon sisters some much needed space from each other, and Chester's male lemurs are in for a shock with a female on her way to share their island(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Spider monkey Mary is 50 and has had 19 children, and the keepers worry that another pregnancy could be risky, so they introduce younger female Kiara to the group in the hope that Mary's long-term partner Popoyan will develop a relationship with her instead. The staff also hope to breed dominant female giant otter Icana with new arrival Diego and plan to bring two feuding sloths closer together, while setting up a speed-dating event for three-legged chameleon Tripod(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick takes a look at 12-year-old cat Pixie, who has been hit by a car and brought in by owner Diane. The vet believes he can help the animal walk again with the aid of a revolutionary bionic implant. A Great Dane called Ellie has deformed bones and discs pressing down on her spinal cord, but Noel hopes to relieve the dog's pain by placing bone marrow taken from her shoulder into a special scaffolding in her neck, helping the vertebrae fuse together. Meanwhile, receptionist Natalie introduces a new arrival to the team(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: A dachshund is brought in by owners Kelly and Daniel to see if anything can be done to repair a deformity in its foot, and Noel Fitzpatrick gives the one-year-old a lesson in doggy discipline along the way. A 10-month-old labrador needs a hip replacement before it can begin training as a medical-alert dog to help its owner with her rare health condition. An eight-year-old shih-tzu has the most extraordinary walk the vet has ever seen, but the operation to fix the dog's spine will be extremely risky, presenting the owners with an agonising decision(n)

programme.desc: Golden poison frog Pablo has lost interest in his partner Maria, so 'bad boy' El Loco is brought in to shake things up. In the chimpanzee habitat, there is a power struggle for the role of dominant female, and baby porcupine Marion fends off the meerkat mob(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Eastern black rhino Zuri gives birth to baby Ike, and sun bears Toni and Millie are having relationship issues. Meanwhile, Bactrian camel Shan-shan is under the weather, so the Chester keepers are trying to work out how to help her(n)

programme.desc: Noel Fitzpatrick and the team deal with two emergency cases in this edition. One of its rear legs is shattered and the vet has to perform reconstructive surgery to rebuild it. Chief, an eight-year-old Northern Inuit, dislocated its hip while chasing a fox. programme.title: 9/11: The Lost Tapes
programme.desc: Audio recordings that offer an insight into the moments leading up to the 9/11 terrorist attacks of 2001, and the reaction of air-traffic controllers and military commands after the hijacked planes crashed into the World Trade Centre. programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May are each challenged to find a classic car at auction for £3,000. Exploring whether they are more fun to drive than modern alternatives, the trio then take the vehicles to Majorca to compete in a rally. Jeremy also test-drives the BMW Z4 and Nissan 370Z, and AC/DC frontman Brian Johnson is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson and James May are challenged to create a TV advert for Volkswagen, but soon fall out as James concentrates on detailed information on fuel economy and Jeremy wants something more exciting. Richard Hammond explores an alternative to Germanic sports saloons and tests the Vauxhall VXR8 Bathurst, and former US chat show host Jay Leno is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car(n)

programme.title: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programme.desc: Not Going Out star and Would I Lie to You? team captain Lee Mack takes over the reins for extended edition of the satirical quiz, with broadcaster Janet Street-Porter and comedian Sara Pascoe joining team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton to poke fun at the stories making the headlines in April 2018(n)

programme.title: Expedition with Steve Backshall

programme.title: Jon Richardson: Ultimate Worrier

programme.title: QI

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?

programme.title: Ancient Black Ops

programme.title: WW2 Air Crash Detectives
programme.desc: The documentary concludes with a look at a 1943 crash in Gibraltar, which killed General Wladyslaw Sikorski, the Prime Minister of Poland's London-based government. Presenter Garth Barnard investigates many of the unanswered questions from the tragedy, including why the pilot was wearing a life jacket and why many of the bodies believed to be on the plane were never found, and asks whether the accident could have actually been an assassination(n)

programme.title: Time Team Specials
programme.desc: Tony Robinson heads to Leicestershire to examine archaeological discoveries at the site of the Battle of Bosworth Field, a confrontation that took place in 1485 and led to Henry Tudor taking the English Crown. Artefacts are unearthed that challenge the traditional interpretation of events, and Tony hears from experts who believe that not only were gunpowder weapons used in far greater numbers than previously thought, but also that the encounter was fought in a different place(n)

programme.title: Time Team Specials
programme.desc: Tony Robinson traces the story of the warrior queen, following an excavation in Norfolk that may hold clues to what happened to her tribe, the Iceni, after its defeat at the hands of the Romans. The presenter visits Caistor St Edmund to investigate whether the Empire crushed Boudica's people or if she unwittingly led them to destruction(n)

programme.title: Hitler's Most Wanted
programme.desc: As Hitler's personal lawyer, Hans Frank twisted the law to help the Führer's agenda, and became known as the Butcher of Poland. James finds out how he became a power-hungry monster(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston investigates whether the Knights Templar hid their treasure, looted from Jerusalem, on an uninhabited island in Mahone Bay, Nova Scotia(n)

programme.title: Nuremberg - Nazis on Trial
programme.desc: Dramatisation of the trial of Luftwaffe chief and designated successor to Hitler, Hermann Goering. The larger-than-life character was renowned for his charisma and flamboyance, but also as one of the most unrepentant and ruthless of all Nazis. Having aimed to use the courtroom as a means of rehabilitating the fascist regime, it was only at a late stage that he was forced to concede defeat. Robert Pugh stars(n)

They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Chris Tarrant invites a line-up of famous faces to take their place in the hot seat in a bid to win as much money as possible for their chosen charities(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman(n)
